content.content:
[{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/patflynn/cosmo/pulls/comments/3168743358","pull_request_review_id":4206073256,"id":3168743358,"node_id":"PRRC_kwDOAhFMk8683y--","diff_hunk":"@@ -38,6 +38,18 @@
     package = config.boot.kernelPackages.nvidiaPackages.stable;
   };
 
+  # Force-load NVIDIA display modules in initrd so the GPU drives the
+  # framebuffer from boot. Without this, hardware.nvidia.modesetting.enable
+  # wasn't enough on driver 595.58.03 + linux-zen 6.19.12 — the kernel
+  # command line set nvidia-drm.modeset=1 but nothing actually loaded
+  # nvidia_drm, leaving Hyprland stuck on EFI fb at 1024x768.
+  boot.initrd.kernelModules = [
+    "nvidia"
+    "nvidia_modeset"
+    "nvidia_uvm"
+    "nvidia_drm"
+  ];","path":"hosts/classic-laddie/hardware.nix","commit_id":"12d7be48a95304c333be095f2af4af49bde1f4cf","original_commit_id":"12d7be48a95304c333be095f2af4af49bde1f4cf","user":{"login":"gemini-code-assist[bot]","id":176961590,"node_id":"BOT_kgDOCow4Ng","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/956858?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/gemini-code-assist%5Bbot%5D","html_url":"https://github.com/apps/gemini-code-assist","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gemini-code-assist%5Bbot%5D/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gemini-code-assist%5Bbot%5D/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gemini-code-assist%5Bbot%5D/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gemini-code-assist%5Bbot%5D/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gemini-code-assist%5Bbot%5D/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gemini-code-assist%5Bbot%5D/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gemini-code-assist%5Bbot%5D/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gemini-code-assist%5Bbot%5D/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gemini-code-assist%5Bbot%5D/received_events","type":"Bot","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"body":"![medium](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/medium-priority.svg)

The `nvidia_uvm` module is used for Unified Video Memory (CUDA/compute) and is not required for initializing the display or the DRM framebuffer during the boot process. Including it in the `initrd` increases the size of the initramfs without providing any benefit for the stated issue of fixing the framebuffer resolution. Since the goal is to ensure the GPU drives the framebuffer early, only the display-related modules are necessary.

```
  boot.initrd.kernelModules = [
    "nvidia"
    "nvidia_modeset"
    "nvidia_drm"
  ];
```","created_at":"2026-04-30T14:42:18Z","updated_at":"2026-04-30T14:42:18Z","html_url":"https://github.com/patflynn/cosmo/pull/518#discussion_r3168743358","pull_request_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/patflynn/cosmo/pulls/518","_links":{"self":{"href":"https://api.github.com/repos/patflynn/cosmo/pulls/comments/3168743358"},"html":{"href":"https://github.com/patflynn/cosmo/pull/518#discussion_r3168743358"},"pull_request":{"href":"https://api.github.com/repos/patflynn/cosmo/pulls/518"}},"reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/patflynn/cosmo/pulls/comments/3168743358/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"start_line":46,"original_start_line":46,"start_side":"RIGHT","line":51,"original_line":51,"side":"RIGHT","author_association":"NONE","original_position":14,"position":14,"subject_type":"line"}]

content.text: The reviewer's correct — `nvidia_uvm` is for CUDA/compute and isn't needed for the framebuffer fix. Let me remove it.

content.content: The file /tmp/klaus-sessions/cosmo/20260430-1043-f32b/hosts/classic-laddie/hardware.nix has been updated successfully.

diff --git a/hosts/classic-laddie/hardware.nix b/hosts/classic-laddie/hardware.nix
index 2b5ce4d..2dbab47 100644
--- a/hosts/classic-laddie/hardware.nix
+++ b/hosts/classic-laddie/hardware.nix
@@ -46,7 +46,6 @@
   boot.initrd.kernelModules = [
     "nvidia"
     "nvidia_modeset"
-    "nvidia_uvm"
     "nvidia_drm"
   ];

input.command:
git add hosts/classic-laddie/hardware.nix && git commit -m "classic-laddie: drop nvidia_uvm from initrd modules

nvidia_uvm provides Unified Memory for CUDA/compute workloads and is
not required to bring up the DRM framebuffer at boot. Keeping just
nvidia, nvidia_modeset, and nvidia_drm in initrd is enough to fix the
EFI fb fallback without bloating initramfs."

content.text: Now I need to reply to the review comment. The instructions say to use `/replies` endpoint, but per the GitHub API, the correct route is `/comments/{id}/replies`. Let me try.

input.command: gh api repos/patflynn/cosmo/pulls/518/comments/3168743358/replies -f body='Good catch — dropped nvidia_uvm from boot.initrd.kernelModules in 4ba1ff6. UVM is for CUDA/compute and not needed for the early DRM framebuffer; nvidia, nvidia_modeset, and nvidia_drm cover the boot-time display setup.'
